questions.explanation: Euclid was a Greek mathematician who lived around 300 BC. He is known as the 'Father of Geometry'.

questions.text: Two distinct lines can intersect at only one point.
questions.explanation: According to Euclid's geometry, two distinct lines can intersect at most at one point.

questions.correctAnswer: A postulate is an assumption that is accepted without proof.
questions.explanation: Postulates (or axioms) are basic assumptions that are accepted as true without requiring proof. They form the foundation of geometry.

questions.text: Things which are equal to the same thing are ________ to each other.
questions.explanation: This is Euclid's first axiom: Things which are equal to the same thing are equal to each other.
